vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1712: pan conv jsonl <conv-id> — first-class transcript path resolution for agents

plan.title: pan conv jsonl <conv-id> — first-class transcript path resolution for agents
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: Transcript JSONL path resolution is duplicated across the dashboard server, the conv-lookup skill's conv-find.py, and ad-hoc agent SQL. The skill copy silently drifted for a month after the session_file column was deprecated (PAN-451) — it kept reading only that NULL column, so conv 2598's transcript was reported as 'no session file recorded' even though it existed. There is no CLI surface that answers 'give me the JSONL path for conversation N': `pan conv show` operates on discovered-session ids, not conversation ids.
narratives.Proposal: Add `pan conv jsonl <conv-id>` (alias `transcript`) backed by one status-aware resolver built on the shared `sessionFilePath()` primitive. It resolves a conversation via claude_session_id + cwd, preserves conv-find.py's glob fallback, and distinguishes ok / expired / unknown with deterministic exit codes. Then point the conv-lookup skill at that command so the duplicated Python resolution logic is deleted and every agent — Claude or not — has one canonical command.

autoDecisions.summary: Command named `jsonl` with `transcript` as an alias.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue offered either name; `jsonl` is the more literal/greppable verb for 'give me the JSONL', and an alias costs nothing while covering the alternative phrasing.

autoDecisions.summary: `--json` mode exits 0 with the status in the payload; plain mode exits 0 on 'ok' and 1 on 'expired'/'unknown'.
autoDecisions.rationale: Mirrors conv-find.py --jsonl plain-mode behavior and matches the existing `pan conv show --json` / `current --json` convention (return 0, data in payload). JSON consumers branch on .status, not the exit code.

autoDecisions.summary: Resolver lives in a new module `src/lib/conversations/transcript-path.ts` with signature `(cwd, claudeSessionId) -> { path, status }`.
autoDecisions.rationale: Keeps the database-typed status logic out of the generic low-level paths.ts; a (cwd, sessionId) signature is trivially unit-testable without a DB and reuses sessionFilePath() for derivation.

autoDecisions.summary: Pi-harness conversations (claude_session_id NULL) resolve to status 'unknown'; Pi transcript resolution is out of scope.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue scopes resolution to claude_session_id + cwd. Reporting 'unknown' is honest; extending to Pi via the transcript adapter is a clean follow-up.

autoDecisions.summary: conv-find.py delegates fully to `pan conv jsonl --json` with no guarded inline-derivation fallback.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue's explicit goal is to REMOVE the duplicated resolution logic and have a single canonical command. A guarded fallback would re-introduce the very drift the issue fixes; a clear error when `pan` is absent is the correct failure mode.

autoDecisions.summary: Skill edits target `sync-sources/skills/conv-lookup/` (tracked source of truth).
autoDecisions.rationale: `.claude/skills/` is gitignored and regenerated by `pan sync` (PAN-1090); editing it would be lost on the next sync.

narrative.Action: Create `src/lib/conversations/transcript-path.ts` exporting `resolveConversationTranscript(cwd: string | null | undefined, claudeSessionId: string | null | undefined): { path: string | null; status: 'ok' | 'expired' | 'unknown' }`. Derive the path with the existing `sessionFilePath(cwd, claudeSessionId)` from src/lib/paths.ts. Resolution order, mirroring conv-find.py's resolve_session_file(): (1) if claudeSessionId is falsy → { path: null, status: 'unknown' }; (2) compute derived = sessionFilePath(cwd, sessionId); if it exists on disk → { path: derived, status: 'ok' }; (3) else glob `~/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl` (one level only) and return the first match as { path: match, status: 'ok' }; (4) else → { path: derived, status: 'expired' }. Add a unit test file under src/lib/conversations covering all four outcomes with a temp HOME / ~/.claude/projects fixture. This helper is the single source of truth for status-aware transcript resolution that the CLI command and (transitively) the skill depend on — keep its status taxonomy and return shape stable.

subItems.title: Exported `resolveConversationTranscript(cwd, claudeSessionId)` returns `{ path: string | null, status: 'ok' | 'expired' | 'unknown' }` and derives via sessionFilePath() from src/lib/paths.ts.
Returns status 'ok' with the absolute derived path when `sessionFilePath(cwd, sessionId)` exists on disk.

When the derived path is absent, returns status 'ok' with the first `~/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl` glob match (one directory level), preserving conv-find.py's fallback for cwd-encoding drift.
Returns 'expired' (path = the expected derived path) when claudeSessionId is set but no JSONL exists on disk or via glob; returns 'unknown' (path = null) when claudeSessionId is null/empty.
Unit tests cover all four outcomes (ok-derived, ok-glob, expired, unknown) using a temp ~/.claude/projects fixture; `npm run typecheck` and the new test pass.

items.title: Add `pan conv jsonl <conv-id>` command (alias `transcript`) + registration + tests

narrative.Action: Add `src/cli/commands/conversations/jsonl.ts` exporting `jsonlAction(convId: string, opts: { json?: boolean })`. Parse convId as an integer (exit 1 with a clear stderr error if non-numeric); look up the conversation with `getConversationById()` (exit 1 'Conversation <N> not found' if missing); call `resolveConversationTranscript(conv.cwd, conv.claudeSessionId)`. Plain mode: on 'ok' print the absolute path to stdout and exit 0; on 'expired' print a Claude-Code-retention message to stderr and exit 1; on 'unknown' print a 'no claude_session_id recorded for this conversation' message to stderr and exit 1. `--json` mode: print one JSON object `{ status, path, conversationId, claudeSessionId, cwd }` to stdout and exit 0 regardless of status. Register the subcommand in `src/cli/commands/conversations/index.ts`: `.command('jsonl <conv-id>').alias('transcript').description(...).option('--json', ...)`, with a description that states the argument is the conversation id (the number in pan.localhost/conv/<N>) — distinct from `pan conv show`. Add `src/cli/commands/conversations/__tests__/jsonl.test.ts` following the show.test.ts harness (seed a conversation row, mock process.exit/console, assert exit codes + output for ok / expired / unknown / not-found across plain and --json modes).

subItems.title: `pan conv jsonl <conv-id>` and its alias `pan conv transcript <conv-id>` are registered under the conversations/conv group with a `--json` option; the description states the argument is the conversation id (the /conv/<N> number).
On status 'ok', prints only the absolute JSONL path to stdout and exits 0.
On 'expired' prints a retention-explaining message to stderr and exits 1; on 'unknown' prints a no-claude_session_id message to stderr and exits 1.
Non-numeric conv-id and not-found conversation each exit 1 with a clear stderr error.
`--json` prints a single object `{ status, path, conversationId, claudeSessionId, cwd }` to stdout and exits 0 for ok/expired/unknown alike (status conveyed in the payload).
jsonl.test.ts covers ok / expired / unknown / not-found in both plain and --json modes (exit codes + output); `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and the test suite pass.

items.title: Point conv-lookup skill at `pan conv jsonl`; remove duplicated Python resolution

narrative.Action: In `sync-sources/skills/conv-lookup/scripts/conv-find.py`, rewrite `resolve_session_file(info)` to shell out to `pan conv jsonl --json <id>` (via subprocess), parse the returned `{ status, path }`, and return `(path, status)`. Delete the now-duplicated `encode_claude_project_dir()` and the inline derive+glob logic that the CLI now owns. Handle the failure modes cleanly: if `pan` is not on PATH (FileNotFoundError) or returns malformed/empty JSON, print a single clear stderr line and treat as 'unknown' — do NOT print a Python traceback and do NOT silently re-implement the removed derivation. Update `sync-sources/skills/conv-lookup/SKILL.md` to document `pan conv jsonl <id>` / `pan conv transcript <id>` as the canonical resolver, note that conv-find.py delegates to it, and note the --json status-vs-exit-code contract. Edit ONLY the sync-sources copy (the `.claude/skills/` copy is a gitignored sync target). Verify by running `python3 scripts/conv-find.py --jsonl <id>` against a live conversation (prints path, exit 0) and a known-expired one (reports expired, exit 1).

subItems.title: conv-find.py's resolve_session_file() resolves the path by calling `pan conv jsonl --json <id>`; the inline encode_claude_project_dir + derive + glob duplication is removed.
conv-find.py degrades gracefully when `pan` is missing or returns non-JSON/non-zero output: a single clear stderr line, no traceback, no silent re-implementation of the removed logic.
SKILL.md documents `pan conv jsonl` / `pan conv transcript` as the canonical resolver, states conv-find.py delegates to it, and explains the --json status-in-payload contract.

Only `sync-sources/skills/conv-lookup/` files are edited (not the gitignored `.claude/skills/` copy); `python3 scripts/conv-find.py --jsonl <id>` still prints the resolved path (exit 0) for a live conv and reports expired/unknown the same way.

test.notes: All quality gates pass: npm run test (5693 tests passed), npm run typecheck (TypeScript strict), npm run lint (ESLint). Tests include regression coverage for projectRootFromWorkspace fix: (1) auto-start round-trip test reproducing worktree shape with .git file and .pan/specs/ directory, verifying that writeAutoStartVBrief → createBeadsFromVBrief no longer returns 0 beads; (2) io.test.ts guard tests confirming pre-promotion draft fallback still works for git worktrees and post-promotion specs resolve from main project root (.pan/specs/), not workspace (.pan/specs/). Two completed beads: fix-resolver (io.ts short-circuit branch removed, regression test added) + guard-tests (pre/post-promotion guards + full suite green).
